channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead Fox in Road, 4th April
item.description:
Dead Fox in Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Howard Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Locals dumping food regularly., 4th April
item.description:
I am writing to report an ongoing issue in the woodland area just off Elmstead Lane, particularly on the side closest to Beaconsfield Road, between the last houses on the woods side and the pathway leading onto Elmstead Lane. Food is being regularly dumped in this area, reportedly to feed foxes. However, this includes raw meat and bones, which poses a significant health risk to dogs and likely attracts rats. My own dog has attempted to eat the food, including bones, which could cause serious and immediate harm. The food is left repeatedly in the same locations. Even when it has been cleared, there is clear evidence of where it has been left. I would be happy to point out the exact spots, as I live very nearby. For clarity, the food is being left on the woodland side, directly beneath the black barrier, rather than on the pavement side. It's on the edge, just within the brambles. I would be so grateful it if this could be investigated and appropriate action taken. Many thanks Paul

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wall section about to fall onto pavement / pedestrian feet, 4th April
item.description:
This section of wall is slowly but surely being pushed over by the tree, and its centre of gravity is almost past tipping point, so it's in danger of falling over and hurting/crushing some unlucky person's feet. Please take corrective action before this happens. There's another section nearby that's not as bad but not far off from the same happening.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In Green Lane, Penge on the NE side of the bridge, are the same blue pen tags on the brown substation doors, the two benches and the advertisement hoarding on the bridge., 4th April
item.description:
In Green Lane, Penge on the NE side of the bridge, are the same blue pen tags on the brown substation doors, the two benches and the advertisement hoarding on the bridge. The tagged bus shelter has been separately reported on FMS for TfL attention

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Penge, Greater London

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Inconsiderate resident littering, 3rd April
item.description:
Not the first time that the residents of this property leave unwanted things on the pavement. This is probably some kind of furniture that not only litters, but is a hazard for pedestrians and especially children. Please remove the soonest possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 178, Upland Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bus shelter extensively pen tagged in Green Lane Penge, westbound, between the metal rail bridge and Parish Lane., 3rd April
item.description:
Bus shelter extensively pen tagged in Green Lane Penge, westbound, between the metal rail bridge and Parish Lane. Tags are on both solid panels, roof and glass panel.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: At 50, Lennard Rd Penge, overhanging rose branches and another bush, combined with the two wheel pavement parking permission, make it difficult to pass this location., 3rd April
item.description:
At 50, Lennard Rd Penge, overhanging rose branches and another bush, combined with the two wheel pavement parking permission, make it difficult to pass this location. I am 6 foot two in shoes and use a walking stick. In rain I use an umbrella, obviously, above my head. Please secure the pruning of all bushes back to the residential property boundary and vertical clearance to 3m. A blind or partially sighted person might walk into the roses and wet shrubbery in rain perhaps suffering a facial injury or flicking off glasses.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lennard Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
